file_insights.key_content: Module-level documentation and app configuration pointer
insights: Comprehensive docstring explaining app purpose and models
Documents critical multi-tenant isolation rule (organizationId required)
Establishes soft delete pattern (deleted_at timestamp)
Documents order status lifecycle: PENDING → CONFIRMED → PREPARING → READY → DELIVERED → COMPLETED
Sets default_app_config for legacy Django compatibility

file_insights.key_content: Django AppConfig class for orders application
insights: Uses BigAutoField as default primary key generator
Verbose name set to 'Order Management'
Ready method prepared for signal registration with commented template
Proper app naming convention: 'apps.orders'
Clean structure with placeholder for future signal imports

file_insights.key_content: Nine TextChoices enums defining constrained string fields
insights: TableStatus: 3 states (AVAILABLE, OCCUPIED, RESERVED)
QRCodeType: 3 types (MENU, TABLE, CAMPAIGN)
OrderStatus: 7 states with documented lifecycle progression
OrderType: 3 fulfillment methods (DINE_IN, TAKEAWAY, DELIVERY)
OrderItemStatus: 5 states with documented lifecycle
ServiceRequestType: 4 request types (WAITER_CALL, BILL_REQUEST, HELP, OTHER)
ServiceRequestStatus: 4 states (PENDING, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, CANCELLED)
PaymentStatus: 5 states (PENDING, PARTIAL, PAID, REFUNDED, FAILED)
PaymentMethod: 6 methods (CASH, CREDIT_CARD, DEBIT_CARD, ONLINE, WALLET, OTHER)
All enums include human-readable labels for admin display

file_insights.key_content: Django admin registration templates and configuration patterns
insights: Comprehensive commented templates for 7 admin classes
Templates follow consistent multi-tenant filtering pattern
Soft delete filtering implemented via deleted_at__isnull=True
Admin classes include: ZoneAdmin, TableAdmin, QRCodeAdmin, OrderAdmin, OrderItemAdmin, ServiceRequestAdmin
Demonstrates fieldset organization for complex Order model
Shows search and filter patterns for different model types
Readonly fields pattern established for audit fields (id, created_at, updated_at)

patterns_discovered.description: Every model includes organizationId and requires filtering by organization
evidence: Module docstring emphasizes 'Every query MUST include organizationId for tenant isolation'
Admin templates show organization filtering in list_filter
Organization appears in search_fields for cross-organization discovery

patterns_discovered.description: All entities use soft deletion via deleted_at timestamp instead of physical deletion
evidence: Module docstring states 'All entities use soft delete (deleted_at timestamp)' and 'No physical deletions allowed'
Admin templates implement get_queryset filtering: qs.filter(deleted_at__isnull=True)
Pattern applied consistently across all admin classes
patterns_discovered.impact: Enables audit trail and data recovery

patterns_discovered.description: Multiple models define documented state progressions
evidence: OrderStatus: PENDING → CONFIRMED → PREPARING → READY → DELIVERED → COMPLETED
Each includes CANCELLED as terminal state
patterns_discovered.impact: Enforces business logic constraints through choice fields

patterns_discovered.description: Admin configuration provided as commented code templates ready for implementation
evidence: admin.py contains 126 lines of structured admin class templates
Each template includes list_display, list_filter, search_fields, readonly_fields
Order admin demonstrates fieldsets pattern for complex forms

patterns_discovered.description: Centralized TextChoices enums for all constrained string fields
evidence: 9 distinct TextChoices classes defined in choices.py
Each enum includes both value and human-readable label
Usage pattern documented in module docstring
patterns_discovered.impact: Single source of truth for field values, prevents duplication

gotchas_discovered.issue: Admin classes are commented out, not registered
gotchas_discovered.explanation: The admin.py file contains templates but no actual @admin.register decorators or registration calls
gotchas_discovered.consequence: Models won't appear in Django admin until these templates are implemented
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Admin classes must be implemented before admin functionality is available

gotchas_discovered.issue: Signal imports are commented out in AppConfig.ready()
gotchas_discovered.explanation: The ready() method has a pass statement and commented signal import
gotchas_discovered.consequence: Any signals defined for the orders app won't be registered until signals module is created and import is uncommented
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Create signals.py module and uncomment import when signal handling is needed

gotchas_discovered.explanation: Only choices, admin templates, and app config exist; actual model definitions are missing
gotchas_discovered.consequence: The architecture is defined but not implemented; cannot run migrations or use the app
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Next subtask must implement models.py with Zone, Table, QRCode, Order, OrderItem, ServiceRequest models

gotchas_discovered.issue: Default app config pattern is legacy
gotchas_discovered.explanation: The default_app_config in __init__.py is deprecated in Django 3.2+
gotchas_discovered.consequence: May generate deprecation warnings in newer Django versions
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Can be removed for Django 3.2+ but current usage is safe for compatibility

gotchas_discovered.issue: Payment-related enums lack corresponding admin templates
gotchas_discovered.explanation: PaymentStatus and PaymentMethod enums are defined but no payment model admin template provided
gotchas_discovered.consequence: Suggests payment handling may be in a separate module or incomplete
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Confirm whether payment models belong to this app or another app

approach_outcome.description: Successfully created orders Django app foundational structure
achievements: Created properly documented AppConfig with BigAutoField default
Defined 9 comprehensive TextChoices enums covering all domain status/type fields
Documented critical multi-tenant and soft-delete architectural patterns
Provided admin interface templates for all planned models
Established clear order and item status lifecycle progressions
approach_outcome.completeness: Foundation layer complete; model implementation remains

recommendations.recommendation: Implement models.py with all 7 planned models (Zone, Table, QRCode, QRScan, Order, OrderItem, ServiceRequest)
recommendations.rationale: Current structure is architecture-only; models must be defined before any functionality can be used

recommendations.recommendation: Uncomment and implement admin.py registration when models are created
recommendations.rationale: Admin templates are prepared and should be activated alongside model definitions

recommendations.recommendation: Implement queryset filtering utilities that enforce organizationId requirement
recommendations.rationale: Critical multi-tenant isolation rule documented but not yet enforced at model level
recommendations.example: Create a custom Manager with default filtering for deleted_at and organizationId

recommendations.recommendation: Create signals.py module to handle timestamp updates and state transitions
recommendations.rationale: AppConfig.ready() has placeholder for signals but module doesn't exist
recommendations.use_cases: Auto-update 'updated_at' on save, enforce status lifecycle constraints

recommendations.recommendation: Create database schema documentation diagram showing model relationships
recommendations.rationale: Module docstring describes relationships between Zone→Table→QRCode→Order but no visual
recommendations.deliverable: Entity relationship diagram showing foreign keys and cardinality

recommendations.recommendation: Remove default_app_config from __init__.py for Django 3.2+ compatibility
recommendations.rationale: Pattern is deprecated but currently functional
recommendations.timing: Can be deferred until Django version upgrade
